summary: The bilateral mandibular processes merge through cartilaginous union forming the lower jawbone; no other structures are involved in their direct combination for creating any facial features beyond contributing indirectly as described but not primarily participating according to medical literature reviewed here focusing strictly on anatomical facts pertinent to human skeletal structure evolution from embryo stage onwards up until postnatal period when full adult morphology has been achieved including complete ossification of all bony elements comprising face & jaws systemically
